result.markdown:
--- title: "Animated Plots with Plotly" author: "Mark Muetz" date: "2026-06-03" categories: [code, plotly, animation] --- Plotly charts are fully interactive — zoom, pan, hover for values — and animations add a time dimension with a built-in scrubber. ## Lissajous figure A Lissajous curve traces $x = \sin(at + \delta),\ y = \sin(bt)$. Animating over the phase offset $\delta$ shows how the shape morphs as the two oscillations drift in and out of phase. ::: {#9d5b3e04 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import numpy as np import plotly.graph_objects as go t = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 500) deltas = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 60) frames = [ go.Frame( data=[go.Scatter( x=np.sin(2 * t + d), y=np.sin(3 * t), mode="lines", line=dict(color="steelblue", width=2), )], name=str(i), ) for i, d in enumerate(deltas) ] fig = go.Figure( data=frames[0].data, frames=frames, layout=go.Layout( xaxis=dict(range=[-1.1, 1.1], zeroline=False, title="x"), yaxis=dict(range=[-1.1, 1.1], zeroline=False, title="y", scaleanchor="x"), width=500, height=500, updatemenus=[dict( type="buttons", showactive=False, buttons=[ dict(label="Play", method="animate", args=[None, {"frame": {"duration": 50}, "fromcurrent": True}]), dict(label="Pause", method="animate", args=[[None], {"frame": {"duration": 0}, "mode": "immediate"}]), ], )], sliders=[dict( steps=[dict(method="animate", args=[[f.name]], label="") for f in frames], transition=dict(duration=0), x=0, y=0, len=1.0, )], ), ) fig.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Spiral build-up A simpler example: a spiral that draws itself point by point. ::: {#e95a7cde .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} theta = np.linspace(0, 4 * np.pi, 200) r = theta / (4 * np.pi) x = r * np.cos(theta) y = r * np.sin(theta) frames = [ go.Frame(data=[go.Scatter(x=x[:k], y=y[:k], mode="lines", line=dict(color="coral", width=2))], name=str(k)) for k in range(2, len(x) + 1, 2) ] fig2 = go.Figure( data=frames[0].data, frames=frames, layout=go.Layout( xaxis=dict(range=[-1.1, 1.1], zeroline=False, showticklabels=False), yaxis=dict(range=[-1.1, 1.1], zeroline=False, showticklabels=False, scaleanchor="x"), width=500, height=500, updatemenus=[dict( type="buttons", showactive=False, buttons=[ dict(label="Play", method="animate", args=[None, {"frame": {"duration": 20}, "fromcurrent": True}]), dict(label="Pause", method="animate", args=[[None], {"frame": {"duration": 0}, "mode": "immediate"}]), ], )], ), ) fig2.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
